title: The Sunday Slow Morning Ritual
subtitle: A short ritual for beginning the week in the home you have, not the one you are waiting for
excerpt: A five-minute ritual for Sunday morning that anchors you in the home you live in now. Moves through the house with deliberate attention before the week starts, as a small act of ownership rather than waiting.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. The five-part ritual structure draws on the ceremony shape in MONEY Journal (Rebecca Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use on Sunday morning, or any morning when the week ahead feels like the real life and the home you are in feels like the temporary version.

content.text: A five-minute Sunday morning ritual for grounding yourself in the home you live in now. The practice is a single slow walk through the house, with one spoken release and one spoken allow, before the week begins.

Before the household is fully awake, make one drink and carry it through each room of the house. You are not tidying, not assessing what needs doing. You are walking through the space as someone who lives there. Notice what is in each room. Notice what you have built or arranged or allowed to accumulate there.
I am ready to release the idea that this home is a temporary version of the real one. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
I am ready to allow this home to be the place where my life is happening. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.
Sit down in one room with your drink. Not at the desk, not at the kitchen table with a screen. Just in the room. Spend three minutes there. Let the house be around you.
This is my home. I live here now.
Say it once. Then get on with the morning.
Written for homemade.education. The five-part ritual structure draws on the ceremony shape from MONEY Journal (Rebecca Page, 2025).
